title: UDLM Rule-ID Registry
description: The single source of truth for normative rule-ID prefixes: one prefix = one rule family = one home file. See registry/rule-id-naming.md and ADR-028.

prefix.description: 2-6 chars, leading letter. The rule family; IDs are `<prefix>-NNN` (hyphen-segmented sub-series like REG-DP resolve to their leading prefix).

home.description: The single repo-relative doc where this prefix's rules are defined.

domain.description: One line: what this family of rules governs.

baseline_spread.description: Grandfathered non-home files where this prefix is still defined (dedup DEBT to burn down). An entry that no longer spreads is reported stale.

additional_homes.description: SANCTIONED co-definition files: a family that deliberately spans more than one doc with a coordinated number space and no duplicate ID number (e.g. GRP across resource-grouping + universal-groups). Permanent, not debt. The duplicate-ID-number invariant is still enforced.
